creator: swills@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Wed, Mar 19, 2014 at 10:51:32PM -0700, Joseph Benden wrote:
> 
> 
> Steve Wills wrote:
> > Is it?
> 
> I swore I had a runtime error (Ruby) when testing before. However, I am
> able to run it now using Ruby 2.0.
> 
> >  Looking at their site, it looks like at least the Mac OS X package and
> > the Debian package are bundling ruby 2.0, not 2.1. Are you sure 2.1 is required?
> 
> I guess not. But I still think it is best to use the newer version of
> Ruby. ;)
> >
> > Build testing the latest shar now, with some slight changes, btw.
> 
> Let me know how this goes, especially if you try Ruby 2.0.

I unfortunately cannot test as I run 11-CURRENT and haven't had any luck
getting VirtualBox working on 11-CURRENT due to a (possibly local) c++ related
lib issue which causes VBox to core dump on startup. I have an updated version
of the shar though, see attached. This at least passes all poudriere build
tests. Can you run test?

Steve
